Thes. 339. when it be notorious that a man have no right to govern , the people be not bind to obey he , unless by accident . else we shall be bind to take every man for our sovereign that call himself so : and so the people shall be bind to treason , and to have many sovereign , when many pretend to it . material man may be oblige to obey ( that be , to do the thing command ) in prudence to avoid a evil to the nation or themselves . but formal obedience be due to none but such as be in authority , or seem to have authority , and so quoad nos be true governor , though perhaps their title ceram deo may not be good . even as i be not to take any man for a pastor of the church that be not lawful call to it , or be in possession and administration do •eem so to the church . Thes. 340. if a lawful king be limit , if he command the subject beyond his limit , in matter exempt from his power , or else in matter that the nature of his office extend not unto , that command be not a act of power ; and therefore it be not a resistance of power to disobey it . the resistance of a person in power , in a point wherein he have no power , be not to resist power , ( that be , jus regendi ) but the will of a private man : for he be a private man in all thing exempt from his power . 1. a schoolmaster have nothing to do to command his scholar in matter about their trade and calling in the world , but only in matter of learning and manner , because it belong not to his office . a captain have no power ( as such ) about man estate , but only about the manner and military action of his soldier , in order to his military end . if a judge of one court step into another without commission , in alieno foro , his sentence be null , and no man bind to obey it . so if the minister presume to command in thing belong to the magistrate , and not to he , his act be private , vain and null . so if a sovereign will turn physician , and command all man to take this or that physic only , not in order to public good , but private health ; or if he will turn pastor and do thing proper to a pastor of the church , his act be private and null , as be without the verge of his vocation . 2. and where his covenant with his people limit he , he have no power in the except point ; e . g . if he be restrain from raise tax without the people consent , if he yet command the payment of such tax , he do it not by authority : for neither god nor man do ever give he authority thereto . if the constitution restrain he from raise war without the consent of the senate , and yet he undertake to do it , it be not a act of authority , for he never have authority thereto . object . London , print by Richard Cotes , 1645. to the right honourable the lord mayor of the city of London ; and to the right worshipful the alderman , his brethren : right honourable , right worshipful , when at first i see this empty pamphlet , entitle , a plea for the commonalty of London , peep out of the press ; much like a glow-worm , proud of some counterfeit light , which it pretend to have ; i imagine it the best way to let it alone , that it may shame it self by show it self , as indeed need no other enemy to overthrow it but its own weakness . but find it since ( with more success than desert ) to scatter a large spawn about the city by a second impression ; and that this paint worm begin to deceive too many dim-sighted traveller : i think fit ( from the service i owe to truth and peace ) and order which be a friend to they both ) to send abroad this seasonable light , which though it be but small , be yet true , and i hope clear enough to discover the other to be no more than a silly worm . the honour i bear to this famous city , and to the preservation of its peace from all seditious rent , which some turbulent sick brain will be ever hatch in it , cause i at this time to present to your wisdom two humble motion : the first as touch this pamphlet , which i now take upon i to answer ; the second as touch the maintenance of that honour and power which God and the kingdom have invest you with , and particular hand can just take from you . as touch the book , i will not aggravate the spirit that it carry , and that carry it along : nor the mischief that such a brat of too rash a brain , may breed ( if not prevent ) to your self and this city . what tumult , uproar , may in all likelihood follow such dare encouragement , in a multitude prick with desire of power , and power of vindicate pretend privilege ? ( i bless God ) it have yet take no such effect . nor will i aggravate the author offence , who ( humble submit to your better judgement : ) i conceive bind by oath not thus desperate to have send abroad ( without order from that court , whereof he be a member ) so dangerous a spark as this be , which though it seem little at the first , if not timely quench , may quick prove a great incendiary . nor last , will i tell you what power lie in your hand to punish such a offender , thus endeavour to disturb the city peace , by disturb the city order and government , and thus ( i have almost say ) violate his sacred oath . my lord , i be not a man of that spirit : it be Caesar honour that he overcome more , and more overcome by his clemency than his sword ; the last overcome but the body of man , the other their mind : it will be your lordship honour , and the rest of your brethren , who be all engage in this quarrel , in respect of the author of this pamphlet , to endeavour as not to lose by he , so if possible to gain he , and if not to more wisdom , to more obedience : but in the mean time i remember how Alexander deal with one of his soldier , who he hear rail at himself , who be his general . though he may have punish he at his pleasure , yet he take only this revenge upon he : he cause proclamation to be make throughout his whole army , that such a soldier have scandalous rail against Alexander . and that therefore for the time to come , he shall have the liberty to say any thing what he list , be it good or bad ; no body at all regard or take notice of what be speak by such a fellow as he . you shall not need to make proclamation what injury the author have do you by this pamphlet ( who be , as it be , one of your soldier , and you his general ) the book it self sufficient show it ; and the answer a little more than the book : let i only commend unto you this heroical resolution ; neither to mind it for the present , nor he for the future , nor whatsoever he shall speak or write , till you find he wise , and let that be his punishment . my second motion which concern your self be this : that you will serious consider , that the honour and power which be confer upon you by charter , and custom , ( the ancient of the two ) though it be in your hand to use , be not in your power to give away : nor will your famous city turn to be any other than a monster of many head , when its renown government , which be now so full establish , and so brave digest into a fit method and order ; shall again be throw into its old chaos , by confuse thrust it into the hand of the multitude , who a great emperor long ago compare to fire and water , that be good servant , but bad master . my lord , i do not any whit hereby charge or asperse those renown member of your own body , the commoner that sit below you : and without who you your self be able to do nothing to any purpose : but as it be in the natural body , so i will have it in this body Politique ;
